jokes.text: Moira frantically screaming about something crawling up her leg that turns out to be invisible
jokes.setup: Moira is panicking about something in the bed crawling up her leg
jokes.punchline: She can't see it - it's 'invisible like lice or scabies or bed bugs'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Moira - her dramatic overreaction to what may be nothing fits her personality. Well-structured escalation from mysterious threat to absurd invisible parasites.

jokes.text: John's deadpan response: 'Oh, we don't have bed bugs' with complete certainty about their terrible accommodations
jokes.setup: Moira is freaking out about bed bugs
jokes.punchline: John casually dismisses the possibility despite them living in a seedy motel
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for John's pragmatic denial. Economic delivery with solid ironic contrast.

jokes.text: Moira's dramatic concern: 'I can't afford to burn all my clothes' - treating lice like a house fire
jokes.setup: Normal response to potential lice would be washing or treatment
jokes.punchline: Moira jumps to burning all her clothes as the only solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - only Moira would think burning clothes is the logical response to lice. Shows her dramatic worldview and vanity perfectly.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's surprise entrance after John's logical 'If we had lice, I'd have lice'
jokes.setup: John is being rational about the lice situation
jokes.punchline: Sudden appearance of Dee Dee interrupts with 'Guess who?' timing
jokes.explanation: Functional comedic entrance but nothing special. Basic misdirection that serves story more than comedy.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's immediate concern: 'Do you really have lice? I'd hate to contract lice'
jokes.setup: Sister just arrived for surprise visit to support fallen family
jokes.punchline: First concern is catching their parasites, not their wellbeing
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Dee Dee - shows her superficial concern and self-preservation. Well-timed interruption of reunion sentiment.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's backhanded explanation: 'I really wanted to come visit when I heard about your life collapsing, but I got a really big job promotion that week'
jokes.setup: Expressing regret about not visiting during crisis
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions their 'life collapsing' while prioritizing her promotion
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - the casual cruelty of 'life collapsing' combined with the selfish excuse perfectly establishes Dee Dee. Great structure with false sympathy leading to brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's dig: 'Thank goodness for the Internet. Seemed to be the only thing letting me know you two were still alive'
jokes.setup: Commenting on lack of communication
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she only knew they were alive through internet news of their downfall
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that shows Dee Dee's casual cruelty. The internet reference suggests she was following their public humiliation for entertainment.

jokes.text: Moira's sarcastic response: 'Telephone is also amazing. You just push the buttons and say, Hi, it's me, your deadbeat sister'
jokes.setup: Dee Dee complaining about lack of communication
jokes.punchline: Moira demonstrates how easy it would have been to call, while calling Dee Dee a deadbeat
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure with Moira turning the tables. The 'deadbeat sister' insult is well-integrated into the fake helpful tone.

jokes.text: John's passive-aggressive introduction: 'Come say hi to your Aunt Dee Dee. Better hurry before she disappears for another decade'
jokes.setup: Normal family introduction to aunt
jokes.punchline: Immediate dig about her long absence disguised as urgency
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for John's understated hostility. Well-structured as helpful direction with buried insult.

jokes.text: Alexis's confused question: 'Why did I think that you were dead?'
jokes.setup: Seeing aunt she hasn't seen in years
jokes.punchline: Genuinely confused about why she thought the aunt was dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - her disconnection from family and casual confusion about death is very in-character. Good absurdist beat.

jokes.text: David's memory: 'I think you crashed one of my pool parties with some guy you were calling Uncle Julio'
jokes.setup: Trying to remember when they last saw each other
jokes.punchline: Specific memory of her crashing his party with inappropriate plus-one
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that establishes Dee Dee's pattern of inappropriate behavior. The 'Uncle Julio' detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: All right, you guys treat yourself, okay? Buy yourself something nice. - $100? - Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee gives money to her niece and nephew
jokes.punchline: Kids' shocked reaction to receiving $100 shows how broke they are
jokes.explanation: Effective contrast showing how far the family has fallen financially.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's accommodation explanation: 'I booked myself into a junior suite at the Comfort Inn in Elmdale... It doesn't have a gym and I'm kind of in the best shape of my life right now'
jokes.setup: Explaining why she won't stay at the motel
jokes.punchline: Rejecting their place while bragging about her fitness routine
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dee Dee's narcissism and insensitivity. The humble-brag about being in great shape while rejecting their accommodations.

jokes.text: Okay, so you're saying that I would be as in shape as you are if I just fixed lights all day?
jokes.setup: Grant claims his handyman work keeps him fit
jokes.punchline: Stevie's skeptical questioning of his fitness claims
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie moment - her deadpan delivery questioning obvious BS.

jokes.text: David calling Grant 'a big, fat liar' about his physique coming from handyman work
jokes.setup: Grant claims his body comes from just doing repairs
jokes.punchline: David's obvious jealousy disguised as calling out dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Shows David's attraction and jealousy. The 'big, fat liar' is comedically petty given the topic.

jokes.text: Grant's awkward exit: 'My head is all over the place. She keeps distracting me'
jokes.setup: Caught between David and Stevie's obvious tension
jokes.punchline: His discomfort at being in the middle of sexual tension
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment showing how obvious the sexual tension is to bystanders.

jokes.text: Stevie's observation: 'Wow. He is wearing a tight shirt'
jokes.setup: After Grant leaves following sexual tension
jokes.punchline: Stevie's typically understated acknowledgment of his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie delivery - deadpan acknowledgment of obvious attraction. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: David's sexually suggestive questions: 'So, is he gonna do some repairs on your... undies?'
jokes.setup: Talking about Grant doing repairs around the motel
jokes.punchline: David making awkward sexual innuendo with the repair theme
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that relies on obvious sexual innuendo. Shows David's awkwardness but the joke itself is lazy.

jokes.text: David's awkward rambling: 'Do you. Do him. Do both. Do things. Yeah.'
jokes.setup: Trying to be supportive of Stevie dating Grant
jokes.punchline: His obvious jealousy making him stumble over words encouragingly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's internal conflict. The repetitive 'do' structure shows his mental struggle.

jokes.text: David's bitter comment about 'infrequent benefits that our friendship has'
jokes.setup: Discussing their friends-with-benefits arrangement
jokes.punchline: David's passive-aggressive emphasis on how infrequent the benefits are
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing David's frustration through sarcasm. The word 'infrequent' does the comedic work.

jokes.text: David's petty timing: 'Can we do that after you screw Mr Fix-it, or would you like to do it now?'
jokes.setup: Discussing when to set relationship boundaries
jokes.punchline: David's jealous sarcasm about her dating Grant
jokes.explanation: Shows David's jealousy through passive-aggression. 'Mr Fix-it' is a decent dismissive nickname.

jokes.text: Twyla's casual mention: 'Your mom did the same thing to me this morning'
jokes.setup: Apologizing for hitting Alexis
jokes.punchline: Reveals Moira also accidentally hit her earlier
jokes.explanation: Good efficient callback showing this is a pattern. Economical delivery that builds the world.

jokes.text: Twyla's father's backstory: 'My dad always said, When in doubt, say it with a song. He was a roadie with Fleetwood Mac and that was the last thing Mick Fleetwood said to him before the band hit him with a restraining order'
jokes.setup: Sweet advice about expressing feelings through song
jokes.punchline: Dark revelation about restraining order from Fleetwood Mac
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with heartwarming setup completely subverted by dark reveal. The specificity of Mick Fleetwood and restraining order elevates it. Perfect misdirection.

jokes.text: He was a roadie with Fleetwood Mac and that was the last thing Mick Fleetwood said to him before the band hit him with a restraining order.
jokes.setup: Twyla explaining her dad's advice about expressing feelings through song
jokes.punchline: Backstory escalates from inspirational quote to restraining order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from sweet father-daughter advice to dark reality. The specificity of Mick Fleetwood and restraining order is perfect.

jokes.text: David's oversharing: 'I tried it once and the guy ripped the guitar out of my hands and he just started smashing it on the ground. Granted, I am tone deaf and he was a super angry Marine'
jokes.setup: Advising against writing songs for romantic partners
jokes.punchline: Specific traumatic memory with Marine adds absurd context
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's romantic disasters. The Marine detail and tone-deaf admission add layers.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's backhanded assessment: 'You strike me as the sort of person that had a hard time in high school'
jokes.setup: Approaching David for help with a student
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting him as part of her reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn - well-meaning but completely tactless. Good cringe setup for the interaction.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's fashion critique: 'Your floral top, for instance'
jokes.setup: Explaining why David would understand being an outcast
jokes.punchline: Using his fashion choices as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency for Jocelyn's tactlessness. Visual gag about David's fashion sense.

jokes.text: David's self-aware response: 'The idea of me life coaching another human being should scare you. A lot.'
jokes.setup: Being asked to mentor a troubled teen
jokes.punchline: David's honest self-assessment of his unsuitability
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness from David. Good character writing that acknowledges his flaws while being funny.

jokes.text: I knew there was a good person in there somewhere, David.
jokes.setup: David tries to refuse the mentoring request
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn completely misinterprets his self-awareness as hidden kindness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn obliviousness - interprets David's warning about himself as evidence of his good heart.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's parting advice: 'Get the quilted, that other stuff will chafe you right up'
jokes.setup: Ending conversation about helping troubled teen
jokes.punchline: Unsolicited toilet paper advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jocelyn moment - inappropriate intimacy and oversharing. Good non-sequitur timing.

jokes.text: Alexis's story about Saudi prince relationship: 'three-month affair... but for the last two months I was trapped in his palace trying to get to an embassy'
jokes.setup: Explaining longest relationship was 'voluntary'
jokes.punchline: Casual reveal that most of it was captivity
jokes.explanation: Peak Alexis - casually revealing an insane backstory as relationship comparison. Perfect escalation from romantic to hostage situation.

jokes.text: Alexis's relationship wisdom: 'if my boyfriend ever described our relationship as fine, I think I would literally...'
jokes.setup: Mutt describing his relationship with Twyla as 'fine'
jokes.punchline: Alexis's horror at such lukewarm description
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about relationship expectations. Shows Alexis's romantic standards despite her chaotic history.

jokes.text: Oh, give me a break. How did you find me?
jokes.setup: Moira is hiding from her sister
jokes.punchline: Moira's exasperated reaction to being discovered
jokes.explanation: Standard avoidance comedy, but fits Moira's dramatic personality.

jokes.text: Well, where else is there to go? And Johnny may have told me.
jokes.setup: Moira asks how Dee Dee found her
jokes.punchline: Points out the town's limited hiding spots, plus Johnny ratted her out
jokes.explanation: Good practical humor about small-town limitations plus Johnny's betrayal.

jokes.text: - Thank you? - Ugh. Too late.
jokes.setup: Moira tries to remember what Dee Dee said when given money
jokes.punchline: Moira's hopeful guess followed by Dee Dee's disgusted rejection
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Moira's attempt at diplomacy shut down immediately.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's justification: 'considering you had over $500 million in the bank, I'm personally offended that I didn't get more'
jokes.setup: Being confronted about not paying back the loan
jokes.punchline: Claiming she deserved more money given their wealth
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Dee Dee's entitlement and greed. Subverts expectation of guilt into more demands.

jokes.text: Didn't want to hurt your feelings, but I forgot. - You don't have feelings.
jokes.setup: Dee Dee claims she didn't want to hurt Moira's feelings
jokes.punchline: Immediately remembers Moira is heartless
jokes.explanation: Excellent sibling cruelty - the fake consideration followed by brutal honesty about Moira's character.

jokes.text: Moira, the woman dates dive-bar owners, okay? Let her insult us.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to convince Moira to take the money
jokes.punchline: Dismisses Dee Dee's opinion based on her dating choices
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's class consciousness and practical priorities.

jokes.text: John's pragmatic plea: 'That cheque could pay for fumigation. Better yet, we could move to a place that doesn't have bed bugs'
jokes.setup: Moira rejecting Dee Dee's money offer
jokes.punchline: John bringing up the bed bug issue from the opening
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening sequence. Shows John's practical desperation overcoming pride.

jokes.text: John's comparison: 'you spent more than that on wigs that year'
jokes.setup: Arguing about the $50,000 loan
jokes.punchline: Putting it in perspective of Moira's wig budget
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Moira's spending priorities. Economical way to show their former wealth.

jokes.text: John's champagne fantasy: 'And then we can buy a case of champagne to wash it down with'
jokes.setup: Telling Moira to swallow her pride
jokes.punchline: Literal champagne to wash down the metaphorical pride-swallowing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning metaphorical 'swallow your pride' into literal champagne consumption. Good structure.

jokes.text: David's observation about Grant: 'Like one of those guys that has like a candy bowl of condoms on his bedside table'
jokes.setup: Describing Grant as respectable
jokes.punchline: Specific image of organized safe-sex preparation
jokes.explanation: Good specific visual image that's both funny and revealing of David's assumptions about 'respectable' guys.

jokes.text: Stevie's response: 'I hope so. Wonder what colour I'll get'
jokes.setup: David's comment about Grant having a condom bowl
jokes.punchline: Stevie's practical interest in condom variety
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery. Economical line that shows her practicality and sexual confidence.

jokes.text: Three point five. I'm gonna leave you be because it's not every day you get to go on a date with a guy who owns a windowless van, so that...
jokes.setup: David rating his enthusiasm for mentoring
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive comment about Grant's creepy vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good jealous David moment - the specific 'windowless van' detail suggests predatory behavior.

jokes.text: Grant's awkward question: 'What're you doing? You checking out porn again?'
jokes.setup: Walking in on David and Stevie's tense conversation
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption about David's computer activity
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor. Shows Grant's lack of social awareness but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: David's overly formal exit: 'Warmest regards to you both. Best wishes.'
jokes.setup: Awkwardly leaving after tense moment with Stevie and Grant
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately formal farewell for casual situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's awkwardness through overly formal language. Classic David defense mechanism.

jokes.text: Grant and Stevie's shared assessment: 'Such a weird dude. Yes. Yes, he is.'
jokes.setup: David's awkward exit
jokes.punchline: Their immediate agreement about his weirdness
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that works but isn't particularly original. Shows their connection through shared observation.

jokes.text: Connor's brutal assessment of David: 'Look at you. Look at your pants.'
jokes.setup: David trying to help as a mentor
jokes.punchline: Teen immediately judging David's appearance as disqualifying
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where the teen turns the tables on David. Economical putdown that references visual comedy.

jokes.text: Connor's self-aware declaration: 'I'm a 16-year-old gay kid living in a town that makes me wanna throw up. The issue isn't me not fitting in. It's me not wanting to fit in.'
jokes.setup: David trying to help him with fitting in problems
jokes.punchline: Connor's mature insight that he chooses not to fit in
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing that subverts expectations. Connor is more self-aware than the adult trying to help him. Good structure with clear logic.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's pork obsession at dinner: 'The ribs, the chops, the roast... but I'm definitely ordering pork'
jokes.setup: Trying to decide what to order at dinner
jokes.punchline: Obsessive focus on pork products while Moira clearly can't eat
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee Dee's insensitivity through food obsession. The repetition builds but could be more economical.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's manipulation: 'I'm just really uncomfortable right now with my older sister acting like a stubborn child'
jokes.setup: Moira refusing to apologize or accept money
jokes.punchline: Dee Dee calling Moira childish while being manipulative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee Dee's manipulation tactics. The irony works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's false modesty: 'I came here in the spirit of giving and forgiving. The gloating thing was a bonus.'
jokes.setup: Claiming pure motives for the visit
jokes.punchline: Admitting gloating was part of the plan
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee Dee's honest cruelty. The 'bonus' framing is well-crafted.

jokes.text: I came here in the spirit of giving and forgiving. The gloating thing was a bonus.
jokes.setup: Defending her motivations for visiting
jokes.punchline: Admits the gloating was indeed part of her plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee Dee honesty - admits to mixed motivations with complete shamelessness. The phrasing is elegant.

jokes.text: Dee Dee's power play: 'I'm gonna do this for you, but mostly, I'm doing it for me. Why? Because you can't and I can.'
jokes.setup: Agreeing to help after Moira's plea
jokes.punchline: Explicitly stating it's about power and superiority
jokes.explanation: Excellent villain moment where Dee Dee drops all pretense. The 'you can't and I can' structure is powerful.

jokes.text: Connor's relationship advice: 'Have you not seen the 42 films they've made about it? It never works.'
jokes.setup: David explaining his friends-with-benefits situation
jokes.punchline: Teen citing movie statistics about relationship failure
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with the exaggerated '42 films' number. Shows Connor's wisdom despite his age.

jokes.text: David's defensive response: 'movies aren't always right... I happen to be a little bit older than you are'
jokes.setup: Being schooled by a teenager
jokes.punchline: David pulling rank on age while clearly being wrong
jokes.explanation: Shows David's insecurity about being outsmarted by a teen. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I have a party later. Can you buy me some beer? Uh, no. Can you buy me some beer?
jokes.setup: David checking if Connor is stable
jokes.punchline: Connor immediately asks for alcohol, David counter-asks for same
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' immaturity - neither should be mentoring the other.

jokes.text: Connor's party request: 'Can you buy me some beer?' and David's counter: 'Can you buy me some beer?'
jokes.setup: Connor asking adult to buy alcohol
jokes.punchline: David turning it around, suggesting Connor seems more mature
jokes.explanation: Good reversal that acknowledges Connor is more together than David. Economical structure.

jokes.text: David's confession: 'I actually hate music'
jokes.setup: Twyla asking if he wants to hear her song
jokes.punchline: David's bizarre claim to hate all music
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - his contrarian nature taken to absurd extreme. Who hates all music? Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Grant's bathroom disappearance story
jokes.setup: Stevie explaining her date with Grant
jokes.punchline: Grant went to bathroom and never came back
jokes.explanation: Classic bad date story. Gets sympathy laughs but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Stevie's alternative theory: 'or Grant has some serious digestive issues'
jokes.setup: Being stood up halfway through date
jokes.punchline: Stevie's optimistic medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - finding the most charitable explanation for being abandoned. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: So this is it? After all that? And we paid for the dinner.
jokes.setup: Looking at Dee Dee's small check amount
jokes.punchline: Johnny's mounting outrage culminating in the practical insult of paying for dinner
jokes.explanation: Good Johnny indignation - the dinner detail shows how completely they were taken advantage of.

jokes.text: The check amount reveal - Dee Dee 'missed a zero' or two
jokes.setup: Expecting substantial payback for $50,000 loan
jokes.punchline: Check is insultingly small amount
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the dinner tension. Visual comedy of their shock at the pathetic amount. Well-structured ending.

jokes.text: She missed a zero. Moira, she missed a zero or two.
jokes.setup: Johnny realizing how small the check is
jokes.punchline: His hopeful interpretation that it's a mathematical error
jokes.explanation: Good desperate Johnny logic - can't believe the amount is intentional, must be a mistake.

jokes.text: John's final assessment: 'Your sister is a cheap-ass'
jokes.setup: Trying to be diplomatic about Dee Dee all episode
jokes.punchline: John finally calling her what she is
jokes.explanation: Satisfying character payoff with John dropping his diplomatic facade. Economical and earned by his restraint all episode.

jokes.text: Your sister is a cheap-ass.
jokes.setup: Moira turns off the light, ending discussion
jokes.punchline: Johnny's final, defeated assessment of Dee Dee
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat - Johnny's crude but accurate summary after all the family drama.
